From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Play written by Sam Shepard

| A Lie of the Mind | |
| --- | --- |
| A scene from the play. | |
| Written by | [Sam Shepard](/wiki/Sam_Shepard "Sam Shepard") |
| Characters | * Baylor * Jake * Lorraine * Mike * Beth * Frankie * Meg * Sally |
| Date premiered | December 5, 1985 |
| Place premiered | Promenade Theater New York City, New York |
| Original language | English |
| Subject | Two families torn apart by tragedy |
| Genre | Drama |

***A Lie of the Mind*** is a [play](/wiki/Play_(theatre) "Play (theatre)") written by [Sam Shepard](/wiki/Sam_Shepard "Sam Shepard"), first staged at the off-Broadway Promenade Theater on 5 December 1985. The play was directed by Shepard himself with stars [Harvey Keitel](/wiki/Harvey_Keitel "Harvey Keitel") as Jake, [Amanda Plummer](/wiki/Amanda_Plummer "Amanda Plummer") as Beth, [Aidan Quinn](/wiki/Aidan_Quinn "Aidan Quinn") as Frankie, [Geraldine Page](/wiki/Geraldine_Page "Geraldine Page") as Lorraine, and [Will Patton](/wiki/Will_Patton "Will Patton") as Mike. The music was composed and played by the North Carolina [bluegrass](/wiki/Bluegrass_music "Bluegrass music") group the [Red Clay Ramblers](/wiki/Red_Clay_Ramblers "Red Clay Ramblers").

Some critics consider the play the conclusion of a quintet that includes Shepard's [Family Trilogy](/wiki/Family_Trilogy "Family Trilogy"): *[Curse of the Starving Class](/wiki/Curse_of_the_Starving_Class "Curse of the Starving Class")* (1976), *[Buried Child](/wiki/Buried_Child "Buried Child")* (1979), and *[True West](/wiki/True_West_(play) "True West (play)")* (1980), plus *[Fool for Love](/wiki/Fool_for_Love_(play) "Fool for Love (play)")* (1983).[[1]](#cite_note-Roudané-1)

## Plot synopsis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=A_Lie_of_the_Mind&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot synopsis")]

Told in three acts set in Montana and California, the story alternates between two families after a severe incident of spousal abuse leaves all their lives altered until the final collision at an isolated cabin. The two families are linked by the marriage of Jake (son of Lorraine and brother of Sally and Frankie) and Beth (daughter of Baylor and Meg and sister of Mike). The play begins with Beth recuperating in her parents' home after a hospitalization resulting from Jake's abuse. Exploring family dysfunction and the nature of love, the play follows Jake as he searches for meaning after his relationship with Beth and her family as they struggle with Beth's brain damage.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Production history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=A_Lie_of_the_Mind&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Production history")]

*A Lie of the Mind* was first produced [Off-Broadway](/wiki/Off-Broadway "Off-Broadway") at the Promenade Theatre on December 5, 1985, closing on June 1, 1986 after 186 performances.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The cast was as follows:[[4]](#cite_note-4)

* Baylor - [James Gammon](/wiki/James_Gammon "James Gammon")
* Jake - [Harvey Keitel](/wiki/Harvey_Keitel "Harvey Keitel")
* Lorraine - [Geraldine Page](/wiki/Geraldine_Page "Geraldine Page")
* Mike - [Will Patton](/wiki/Will_Patton "Will Patton")
* Beth - [Amanda Plummer](/wiki/Amanda_Plummer "Amanda Plummer")
* Frankie - [Aidan Quinn](/wiki/Aidan_Quinn "Aidan Quinn")
* Meg - [Ann Wedgeworth](/wiki/Ann_Wedgeworth "Ann Wedgeworth")
* Sally - [Karen Young](/wiki/Karen_Young_(actress) "Karen Young (actress)")

### Revival

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=A_Lie_of_the_Mind&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Revival")]

The first major Off-Broadway revival of *A Lie of the Mind* was staged by the [New Group](/wiki/The_New_Group "The New Group") at the Acorn Theatre.[[5]](#cite_note-nyt-5)[[6]](#cite_note-playbill-6) [Ethan Hawke](/wiki/Ethan_Hawke "Ethan Hawke") directed the production with an ensemble cast featuring [Keith Carradine](/wiki/Keith_Carradine "Keith Carradine") (Baylor), [Josh Hamilton](/wiki/Josh_Hamilton_(actor) "Josh Hamilton (actor)") (Frankie), [Marin Ireland](/wiki/Marin_Ireland "Marin Ireland") (Beth), [Laurie Metcalf](/wiki/Laurie_Metcalf "Laurie Metcalf") (Meg), [Alessandro Nivola](/wiki/Alessandro_Nivola "Alessandro Nivola") (Jake), [Maggie Siff](/wiki/Maggie_Siff "Maggie Siff") (Sally), [Frank Whaley](/wiki/Frank_Whaley "Frank Whaley") (Mike), and [Karen Young](/wiki/Karen_Young_(actress) "Karen Young (actress)") (Lorraine; Sally in the 1985 production).[[5]](#cite_note-nyt-5)[[7]](#cite_note-7) The show began previews on January 29, 2010, with a limited engagement from February 18 to March 20, 2010.[[6]](#cite_note-playbill-6) It was nominated for five [Lucille Lortel Awards](/wiki/Lucille_Lortel_Awards "Lucille Lortel Awards") including Outstanding Revival,[[8]](#cite_note-8) and two [Drama Desk Awards](/wiki/Drama_Desk_Awards "Drama Desk Awards"), including [Outstanding Director of a Play](/wiki/Drama_Desk_Award_for_Outstanding_Director_of_a_Play "Drama Desk Award for Outstanding Director of a Play").[[9]](#cite_note-9) The cast was featured on *The New Yorker'*s list of the Best Performers of 2010.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

A revival in the UK was staged at the Southwark Playhouse in 2017,[[11]](#cite_note-11) receiving excellent notices including 5 Stars from *The Times* of London.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

A [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago") revival was staged at Raven Theatre in 2025,[[13]](#cite_note-13) receiving excellent notices including a recommendation from the *[Chicago Tribune](/wiki/Chicago_Tribune "Chicago Tribune")*.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Awards and nominations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=A_Lie_of_the_Mind&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Awards and nominations")]

* 1986 [Drama Desk Award](/wiki/Drama_Desk_Award "Drama Desk Award") for Outstanding Play
* 1986 [New York Drama Critics' Circle Award](/wiki/New_York_Drama_Critics%27_Circle_Award "New York Drama Critics' Circle Award") for Best Play
* 1986 [Outer Critics Circle Award](/wiki/Outer_Critics_Circle_Award "Outer Critics Circle Award") for Best Off-Broadway Play
* 2010 [Lucille Lortel Awards](/wiki/Lucille_Lortel_Awards "Lucille Lortel Awards") for Outstanding Revival (nominee)
* 2010 Drama Desk Award, Outstanding Director of a Play, Ethan Hawke (nominee)
* 2010 Drama Desk Award, Outstanding Music in a Play, Gaines (nominee)
* 2010 [Obie Award](/wiki/Obie_Award "Obie Award") Performance, Laurie Metcalf (winner)
